mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.33% indicates restrictive cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal: each 1% decline in PAYEMS from its 12-month peak predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through reduced earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with PAYEMS decline magnitude (Spearman rho > -0.15).

mechanism_narrative: When real retail sales (RSAFS) decline by more than 2% month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously rises above 4.2%, this joint deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and labor market stress. The interaction of these two signals produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (interaction term p-value > 0.10 in logistic regression).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1.5% annualized over a 3-month rolling window signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability expansion. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market participants price in earnings strength.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in high INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1200 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.0% annualized, signaling demand-side weakness across both residential investment and overall economic activity. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1200K units AND GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15 in multivariate regression).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3.5% month-over-month while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 4.5%, the central bank is perceived as behind the inflation curve. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real rates compress and inflation expectations destabilize equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in a logistic model controlling for baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling accelerating labor market deterioration. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward due to reduced consumer spending and corporate cost pressures.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes > 0.4pp are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously contracts, signaling synchronized supply and labor weakness. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as market participants price in recession risk and earnings compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.0% MoM AND PAYEMS contracts MoM
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (interaction p-value > 0.15 in logistic regression).
